title: Unchecked Initialization Delegatecall in Constructor
reason: Generic constructor delegatecall is the intended proxy initialization mechanism. The reportable issue here is the specific admin-ordering hazard captured in F-001, not delegatecall-based storage writes by themselves.

title: Unchecked Delegatecall in upgradeToAndCall
reason: `upgradeToAndCall` is supposed to delegate into the new implementation for initialization, and only the trusted admin can invoke it. Without the transparent-admin role-stranding condition captured in F-002, this is not a standalone vulnerability.

title: Upgrade to Non-Contract Address After Initial Check
reason: False positive. `upgradeTo` calls `_upgradeTo`, which calls `_setImplementation`, and `_setImplementation` explicitly rejects non-contract addresses at `UpgradeabilityProxy.sol:69-75`.

title: Missing Zero Address Check in changeAdmin
reason: False positive. `changeAdmin` explicitly requires `newAdmin != address(0)` at `AdminUpgradeabilityProxy.sol:77-80`.

title: Unchecked Return Value in upgradeToAndCall
reason: False positive. The code checks the only relevant value, `success`, and initializer return data is intentionally ignored.

title: No Initialization Guard in Proxy
reason: False as stated. This proxy constructor always sets the implementation and rejects non-contract targets, so the implementation slot cannot remain unset through the provided deployment path.

title: isContract Returns False During Constructor
reason: This is a documented OpenZeppelin limitation of `extcodesize`, not a codebase-specific exploit in the reviewed proxy flows.

title: Potential Reentrancy in sendValue
reason: `Address.sendValue` is not used by the reviewed contracts, so this is only a generic library warning rather than a vulnerability in this codebase.
